world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 26
major_event: Day twenty-six: Ironmaul's archivists surface a reference in the ancient boring surveys — the Listener Below is named in three pre-founding inscriptions, predating Lostfeld's establishment. Kessa's fever spikes at dawn. The clan elders debate whether the entity can be severed without killing the host. Ashveil is excluded from the session. Rowen finds a notation in an extended chart margin that may be a third map point. Caeris receives the Farrock emergency dispatch.

primary_event.name: Lostfeld Archives Surface the Listener Below — Pre-Founding Inscriptions Confirm Ancient Presence
primary_event.summary: By dawn the clan archivists had worked through the night. At first light they brought Ironmaul three rubbing-copies of pre-founding bore-survey inscriptions, two from the deepest eastern shafts and one from a collapsed western gallery sealed in the third generation. All three use the same notation cluster: a word that translates roughly as the one who receives weight from below. The archivists confirm this predates Lostfeld's founding by at least two centuries. The entity Varek named was here before the Dwarves built above it.

supporting_events.name: Kessa's Fever Spikes — The Binding Responds to the Naming
supporting_events.summary: At dawn Kessa Stonebraid's breathing, which had shifted slightly the previous day, worsened into a low fever. The garrison physician noted her skin had taken on a faint mineral sheen near the original glyph wound site. The Seer, observing from the doorway as Ironmaul's medical watch continued, felt the cold pull intensify when the physician touched the site. Whether the fever is biological stress or the entity responding to its name being spoken aloud, neither the physician nor the Seer can say with certainty.

supporting_events.name: Rowen Finds a Third Chart Notation — Deep-Sea Convergence Zone Becomes Real
supporting_events.summary: Rowen spent the pre-dawn hours re-examining the extended chart margins he had dismissed as marginal notation. One sequence in the far northeast edge of the chart uses the same encoding as the two confirmed map points. It places the third point beyond the northern current boundary — a location unreachable by conventional sail in winter but reachable by a fleet willing to push the season. Rowen is now certain. He updated his private log with three words only: the triangle closes.

supporting_events.name: Caeris Receives the Farrock Dispatch — Northern Convergence Enters Twin Cities Awareness
supporting_events.summary: By midmorning a fast rider from Farrock delivered the emergency dispatch to Regency Hall. Caeris read it alone before his scheduled Halveth consultation: Lostfeld sealed, no external access, Monastery delegation inside, Gnome pass traffic elevated, Seer last known at the garrison. He set the Tidefall condition letter beside it. Two diplomatic pressures arriving in the same morning. He told his steward to delay the Tidefall response by one day and sent Halveth a revised meeting notice for midday.

active_events.name: Varek Detained — Listener Below Confirmed in Pre-Founding Records, Kessa Critical
active_events.summary: Three pre-founding bore inscriptions name the Listener Below, predating Lostfeld by two centuries. Kessa's fever is spiking. Ironmaul convened the senior three elders at first light. Varek remains formally detained. Ashveil is excluded from the archive session. The question of whether severing the binding will kill Kessa is now the council's primary debate.
active_events.consequences: Ironmaul must decide whether Varek's crime is against Lostfeld, against doctrine, or against something older than both. Ashveil's Stonebreak message is already in transit. If Kessa dies, the diplomatic and criminal stakes escalate simultaneously. The Seer is the only outsider present who knows about the three additional Concordance notations.

active_events.name: Rowen's Fleet — Third Map Point Located, Course Decision Imminent
active_events.summary: Rowen has confirmed a third map point in the extended chart margin. The triangle closes on a deep-sea convergence zone north of the Dreadwind Isles, beyond the winter current boundary. He has not yet ordered a course change. Tidefall's two-vessel observation continues. Navigator Briggs has been called to the cabin.
active_events.consequences: A course toward the third point will take Rowen into waters Tidefall cannot easily follow in winter. If he moves without warning, Tidefall loses its observation window. If the Admiral orders intercept before Rowen moves, the council may challenge the order's legitimacy.

active_events.name: Shadow Court — Arkenor's Double Game, Three Days to Meeting
active_events.summary: Meeting is now three days away. Arkenor's Velorn courier is one day out from Velorn's estate. Lythara has received the updated Tidefall prophet intelligence but not yet learned of the Velorn acceleration. The Gnomish syndicate is calculating the price based on what Arkenor has already shown them he values.
active_events.consequences: If Velorn accepts Arkenor's proposal before the meeting, Arkenor's leverage shifts. If Lythara learns of the Velorn acceleration before the meeting, the entire architecture collapses. The Gnomish syndicate is the only party that currently holds all the information.

active_events.name: Frostvale Gate — Gilgeth Reply Unsigned, Physician's Record Growing
active_events.summary: The First Elder held the reply overnight. Morning brought no signature. Frostvale's physician completed a preliminary written report describing two orc crew chiefs exhibiting opioid withdrawal — muscle tremors, sweating, aggression cycles, and sleep disruption. The caravan master in the outer yard has now been in Frostvale for six days. His silence is becoming its own evidence.
active_events.consequences: Every unsigned day hardens the diplomatic record. Frostvale's physician report, if forwarded to external authorities, becomes a formal evidentiary document. The trusted elder coalition has called a second session for this afternoon. The caravan master is beginning to understand his exposure.

active_events.name: The Admiral and the Prophet — Infrastructure Confirmed, Council Pressure Building
active_events.summary: The vampire operative's report of two unmarked crates arriving at the cargo agent's warehouse reaches Lythara today. The second unidentified individual from the warehouse was glimpsed briefly at the morning dock exchange — a woman of middle years, dressed as a cloth merchant. The Admiral has not yet disclosed the prophet network to the council. A council member noted publicly this morning that the harbor district had seen unusual foot traffic for three consecutive nights.
active_events.consequences: The Admiral's silence is becoming a liability as other council members begin noticing independently. Lythara now has the infrastructure picture and may attempt to insert her own operative into the prophet network. The cloth merchant identity is unconfirmed but the vampire operative has her description.

active_events.name: Caeris and the Northern Picture — Twin Pressure from Tidefall and Farrock
active_events.summary: Caeris now holds two simultaneous pressures: the Tidefall condition demanding borderland military suspension during the delegation window, and the Farrock emergency dispatch confirming a sealed Lostfeld garrison with northern faction convergence. He delayed the Tidefall reply by one day. Halveth has been summoned for midday consultation. The High Cleric is also expected.
active_events.consequences: Caeris's decision on the Tidefall condition will signal his political posture to every house watching. Rejecting it signals confidence; accepting it signals deference. The northern picture from Farrock may give him reason to delay further, framing inaction as prudent intelligence-gathering.

active_events.name: Vilefin Tinker — En Route to Farrock with Intelligence Composite
active_events.summary: The tinker and the Farrock courier are one day into their road south. The tinker has organized his composite into a presentable summary. The cipher fragment remains concealed in a false-bottomed pack. The tinker has begun rehearsing what he will say to the garrison commander and how much he will withhold.
active_events.consequences: The tinker arrives at Farrock tomorrow. His intelligence composite will add another layer to Caeris's northern picture when Farrock forwards it. The cipher fragment is a second-sale item the tinker intends to price based on how the first exchange goes.

active_events.name: Dur Khadur — Senior Broker Expanding Northern Picture
active_events.summary: The senior intelligence broker is expanding the preliminary composite. A discreet commercial inquiry through a Farrock intermediary was authorized yesterday and is now in motion. The Dark Council flagged the northern convergence as a priority monitoring situation. No action ordered but the broker has requested authorization for a commercial probe toward the Lostfeld trade corridor.
active_events.consequences: If Dur Khadur establishes a commercial presence in the northern corridor before the garrison unseals, they may intercept trade intelligence before other factions. The Farrock commander's response to the commercial inquiry will determine Dur Khadur's next move.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul convened the senior three elders at first light with the archive rubbing-copies on the table. The session lasted three hours. The council reached one conclusion and one only: the Listener Below predates Lostfeld. This is not a Monastery problem — it is a Lostfeld problem that the Monastery exploited. Ironmaul has not yet told Varek what the archives found. He ordered the archivists to cross-reference the three inscriptions against the current mine shaft maps to determine how close active shafts are to the inscription locations. He also authorized a restricted medical intervention: the garrison physician may attempt to stabilize Kessa's fever using standard means but may not attempt any binding-disruption without council approval.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul needs to know the geography of the entity before he decides what to do about the entity. If the inscription locations align with active mine shafts, the risk calculus changes entirely.
faction_actions.target: Senior elders — session held, archive conclusions reached; archivists — cross-referencing inscriptions against current shaft maps; Varek — not yet told of archive findings; Kessa — fever stabilization authorized, binding intervention held

faction_actions.action: Ashveil was excluded from the morning archive session. She spent the morning alone in her assigned quarters writing a second private document — not for Stonebreak, but a personal record of everything she knows about the Listener Below, the three Concordance notations, and Varek's methodology. She has not yet decided what to do with it. When the Seer passed her door at midmorning, she opened it and asked him directly: does the name the entity was given matter to what it is, or only to how it is addressed? She offered no answer. She is waiting for his.
faction_actions.reason: Ashveil is increasingly uncertain whether Stonebreak will treat this as an institutional crisis or a doctrinal opportunity. Her private document is insurance. The question to the Seer is genuine — she does not know the answer.
faction_actions.target: Private document — being written, not yet shared; Seer — direct question posed; Stonebreak communication — already dispatched, no addendum sent; archive session — excluded, aware of exclusion

faction_actions.action: The Seer heard Ashveil's question at midmorning and sat with it for a long time before answering: he believes naming a thing gives it a shape it lacked before — not more power, but more presence. He has noted in his journal that the cold pull has direction now, not just weight. It comes from below and slightly east — toward the collapsed western gallery marked on the oldest bore maps. He told Ironmaul this in a private moment before the midday meal: not a recommendation, only an observation. He has asked the garrison physician to let him sit with Kessa for one hour.
faction_actions.reason: The Seer cannot act on the Listener Below directly. But he can observe, map, and report. The directional quality of the cold pull may be evidence that the entity has a physical locus — and if it does, that changes what severance would require.
faction_actions.target: Ashveil — answered her question directly; Ironmaul — directional pull observation shared privately; Kessa — sitting with her requested; journal — updated with directional observation; collapsed western gallery — flagged as possible entity locus

faction_actions.action: Rowen called Navigator Briggs to his cabin at dawn and showed him the third map point without explanation, asking only: can we reach it before the winter current locks the northern passage? Briggs studied the chart for twenty minutes and answered: yes, but only if they move within two days. Rowen thanked him and sent him back to the navigation deck. He has not yet ordered the heading change. He spent the afternoon alone with his pipe, watching the Tidefall observation vessels through the stern glass. He is waiting for something — weather, instinct, the right moment — before he commits.
faction_actions.reason: Rowen is certain of the destination. He is not certain of the cost. Moving toward the third point means leaving Tidefall's observation range and potentially triggering intercept. He is calculating whether Tidefall will move first.
faction_actions.target: Navigator Briggs — third point revealed, feasibility confirmed; fleet — northward drift maintained, no heading change yet; Tidefall vessels — observed at distance; decision window — two days before winter current locks passage

faction_actions.action: The Admiral received the vampire operative's full overnight report at dawn: cargo agent warehouse confirmed active, two unmarked crates, disciplined delivery crew, a cloth merchant woman at the morning dock exchange. He reviewed it privately and said nothing at the morning council briefing beyond routine fleet positioning. A council member — Isolde Ver Meer, harbor magistrate — raised the question of unusual harbor district foot traffic independently. The Admiral acknowledged it mildly and said harbor watch would investigate. He has now tasked the harbor watch commander with a separate quiet inquiry that keeps his own name off the instruction.
faction_actions.reason: The Admiral is managing his exposure. Isolde Ver Meer noticing independently means the council is developing its own awareness. He needs to appear ahead of the information without revealing he was already behind it.
faction_actions.target: Vampire operative — report received, acknowledged; harbor watch commander — tasked with quiet inquiry, Admiral's name removed; Isolde Ver Meer — acknowledged publicly, deflected; council — routine briefing maintained; Rowen — fleet status monitored

faction_actions.action: Lythara received the vampire operative's updated intelligence at midmorning — cargo agent as prophet financial intermediary, two unmarked crates, cloth merchant woman at the docks. She spent an hour reviewing it and then issued two instructions: the operative is to attempt visual identification of the cloth merchant woman by end of day, and a Shadow Court courier is to be dispatched toward Tidefall harbor with a low-profile insertion brief — one operative to be placed in the harbor district as a possible prophet-adjacent contact. She has still not received word of Arkenor's Velorn acceleration. Her Arkenor intelligence is one full day behind.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara treats the prophet network as a potential asset. A harbor-based belief movement with financial infrastructure and disciplined logistics is not a street rumor — it is an organization. She wants a node inside it.
faction_actions.target: Vampire operative — cloth merchant identification tasked; Shadow Court courier — dispatched toward Tidefall with insertion brief; prophet network — targeted for infiltration; Arkenor — no new intelligence, one day behind events

faction_actions.action: The syndicate council reviewed the Velorn courier timing and calculated that Arkenor's proposal will reach Velorn's estate by tomorrow morning. They have three days before the meeting. The council prepared a preliminary price framework: access to the Faerwood eastern pass in exchange for Arkenor's formal endorsement of syndicate glass-monopoly exemptions in Shadow Court trade zones. They also dispatched a secondary pass station keeper to shadow Arkenor's travel route toward the meeting waystation, logging any other courier traffic that departs Arkenor's estate in the next two days.
faction_actions.reason: The syndicate wants to know whether Arkenor sends any further couriers before the meeting — a third track would tell them the meeting price needs to rise. Their price is already calibrated to his known interests.
faction_actions.target: Price framework — prepared, not yet revealed; Velorn courier — timing tracked; secondary keeper — dispatched to shadow Arkenor's route; meeting — three days away; Lythara — still not notified

faction_actions.action: Caeris held his midday consultation with Halveth and the High Cleric. He laid both documents on the table — the Tidefall condition and the Farrock northern dispatch. Halveth read both and advised caution on the Tidefall reply: accept the suspension nominally but define borderlands narrowly enough to preserve operational flexibility. The High Cleric expressed concern about the northern convergence and asked whether the Seer's presence at Lostfeld was known officially. Caeris confirmed it was rumor only. He authorized Halveth to draft a careful reply to Tidefall. He sent a private inquiry to Farrock requesting any further intelligence updates.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is threading two pressures simultaneously. The Halveth-framed reply to Tidefall preserves optionality. The Farrock inquiry signals he is taking the northern picture seriously without committing public resources.
faction_actions.target: Tidefall response — being drafted by Halveth, acceptance framed narrowly; Farrock — private intelligence inquiry sent; High Cleric — briefed on northern picture; borderlands military posture — nominally reviewed, no actual change

faction_actions.action: Elowen's Wayfarer observer at the northern mountain approaches sent a second report: a single rider departed the garrison's outer perimeter at speed two evenings ago — direction south-southwest, consistent with a courier heading toward Stonebreak. Elowen logged this as confirmation that the garrison has sent communication outward. She elevated the northern situation from elevated awareness to formal monitoring and tasked a second Wayfarer to the approaches. She has not yet received a response from her own Stonebreak inquiry.
faction_actions.reason: The courier departure confirms the garrison is not simply sealed for internal reasons — it is communicating with Stonebreak, which means the Monastery is involved. Elowen is building her institutional record before events force a decision.
faction_actions.target: Wayfarer observer — second report received, courier departure confirmed; second Wayfarer — dispatched to northern approaches; northern situation — elevated to formal monitoring; Stonebreak inquiry — no response yet

faction_actions.action: The senior broker's expanded composite now includes: Seer at Lostfeld garrison, Monastery delegation inside, Gnome pass traffic elevated at both northern access points, Farrock courier blocked and now returning, single garrison courier departing southward two days ago. The Dark Council's senior member authorized a commercial probe toward the Lostfeld trade corridor — a Dur Khadur factor was dispatched toward Farrock to establish a trading relationship with the garrison commander before the garrison unseals. The factor carries a letter of commercial introduction and no formal political brief.
faction_actions.reason: Dur Khadur's strategy is to be positioned commercially before the garrison reopens. The factor is not an intelligence agent — he is a legitimate merchant, which gives him plausible presence and a cover for observation.
faction_actions.target: Intelligence composite — expanded, senior broker continuing; Farrock — commercial factor dispatched with letter of introduction; Lostfeld corridor — commercial probe authorized; Dark Council — updated briefing scheduled for tomorrow

faction_actions.action: The second session convened at midday under pressure from the trusted elder coalition. The First Elder Hargan Stonejaw finally signed the formal reply to Frostvale — not the admission the trusted elder coalition wanted, but a carefully worded statement acknowledging the crew chiefs' condition as a medical matter requiring joint investigation and offering to send a Gilgeth physician to Frostvale within three days. He also, privately and without council authorization, sent a short message to the caravan master in Frostvale's outer yard: return home by your own means immediately. Do not contact the council directly.
faction_actions.reason: Stonejaw is attempting to contain the situation before the physician's report becomes a public document. The joint-investigation framing buys time. The private message to the caravan master is a mistake — it creates a record of unauthorized contact.
faction_actions.target: Frostvale — formal reply signed and dispatched; caravan master — private unauthorized message sent; trusted elder coalition — partially satisfied by the signing; Groth — not yet involved; physician — offer to send within three days

faction_actions.action: The tinker and the Farrock courier made good time on the southern road. The tinker spent the evening camp organizing his composite into three tiers: what he will offer freely, what he will trade for a letter of introduction, and what he will sell separately for coin. The cipher fragment is in the third tier. He is preparing his approach to the garrison commander for tomorrow morning and has decided to present the Tidefall harbor intelligence as his lead item — it is the most surprising and therefore the most valuable in his estimation.
faction_actions.reason: The tinker's intelligence instinct is reasonable: surprise buys credibility in a first meeting. The cipher fragment held back gives him a return-visit reason if the first exchange goes well.
faction_actions.target: Intelligence composite — organized into three tiers; cipher fragment — concealed for separate sale; Farrock garrison commander — presentation prepared for tomorrow; Tidefall harbor intelligence — chosen as lead item

faction_actions.action: The garrison commander dispatched a reply to Caeris's private inquiry — a second detailed northern update, noting the single courier departure from the garrison two days ago and the expected arrival of the associate intelligence source tomorrow. He also responded to the Dur Khadur commercial factor's advance inquiry through a local trading intermediary: he acknowledged the inquiry and agreed to meet the factor at the garrison's outer market when he arrives, which should be in two to three days. The commander is building his position as the northern information clearinghouse.
faction_actions.reason: Farrock's commander is politically entrepreneurial. Caeris's continued engagement and Dur Khadur's commercial interest together confirm the northern intelligence picture has value. He is not yet choosing sides — he is selling access.
faction_actions.target: Caeris — second northern update dispatched; Dur Khadur factor — meeting acknowledged, arrival expected in two to three days; associate intelligence source — arrival expected tomorrow; intelligence clearinghouse position — being established

faction_actions.action: The Tidefall operative spent the morning at the dock exchange and successfully identified the cloth merchant woman by early afternoon — she is registered in the harbor trade ledger as a wool broker named Osta Rein, but her account was opened only eleven days ago. He confirmed her meeting with the cargo agent lasted approximately twenty minutes. He dispatched his identification report to Lythara and a separate summary to the Admiral's private courier drop. He is now running surveillance on both the cargo agent's warehouse and Osta Rein's registered trading address.
faction_actions.reason: The operative is providing value to both principals simultaneously, which is the nature of his position. Both reports are accurate and neither is complete — the Admiral gets the identification, Lythara gets the institutional profile.
faction_actions.target: Osta Rein — identified, wool broker registration eleven days old, surveillance begun; cargo agent — continued surveillance; Lythara — full identification report dispatched; Admiral — identification summary dispatched to private drop; harbor watch — unaware of operative's dual reporting

recent_events.text: Clan archivists surfaced three pre-founding bore inscriptions naming the Listener Below — the entity predates Lostfeld by two centuries. Kessa's fever spiked at dawn with a mineral sheen appearing near the glyph wound. Ironmaul ordered shaft cross-referencing against inscription locations. The Seer reported a directional quality to the cold pull: below and east, toward the collapsed western gallery. Ashveil was excluded from the archive session and asked the Seer directly about the nature of naming.

Rowen confirmed the third map point in extended chart margins. Navigator Briggs assessed a two-day window before the winter current locks the northern passage. Rowen has not yet ordered the heading change. He watched Tidefall's two observation vessels through his stern glass for an hour at dusk.

Caeris received the Farrock emergency dispatch at midmorning confirming the sealed garrison and northern convergence. He delayed the Tidefall response by one day and summoned Halveth and the High Cleric for a midday consultation. Halveth advised nominal acceptance of Tidefall's condition with a narrowed definition of borderlands. A private inquiry has been sent to Farrock for further intelligence.

Vampire operative identified the cloth merchant woman as Osta Rein, a wool broker whose harbor ledger account is only eleven days old. The Admiral tasked harbor watch with a quiet inquiry while keeping his own name off the instruction. Isolde Ver Meer raised the harbor foot traffic question publicly at morning council.

The First Elder signed the formal reply to Frostvale — a joint-investigation framing, not an admission. A private unauthorized message was sent to the caravan master ordering immediate return. The trusted elder coalition accepted the signing but remains unsatisfied with its wording.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Entity Older Than the Fortress, Kessa Deteriorating
active_tensions.description: The Listener Below predates Lostfeld. The archive inscriptions have shattered the assumption that this was a Monastery import. Kessa's fever is spiking. Ironmaul must now decide whether severance is a Lostfeld decision, a Monastery decision, or something neither institution is equipped to make alone. Ashveil is excluded and increasingly uncertain of Stonebreak's intentions.

active_tensions.factions: Rowen and Tidefall — Two-Day Window, Intercept Decision Approaching
active_tensions.description: Rowen has a confirmed third map point and a two-day feasibility window before the winter current closes. Tidefall's two observation vessels cannot follow into northern winter waters. The Admiral's passive observation posture was designed for a drifting fleet — it was not designed for a fleet that has found what it was looking for.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Arkenor's Velorn Courier Arriving Tomorrow, Lythara Still Blind
active_tensions.description: Arkenor's courier reaches Velorn's estate by tomorrow morning. The Gnomish syndicate is shadowing Arkenor's route. Lythara is one full day behind events and has tasked her network toward Tidefall prophet infiltration rather than the northern Arkenor situation. The meeting is three days away and the price is being calculated without her knowledge.

active_tensions.factions: Gilgeth — Unauthorized Caravan Master Message, Physician Report Hardening
active_tensions.description: Stonejaw's private message to the caravan master was unauthorized and creates a record of attempted contact outside council process. Frostvale's physician is building a written medical record that will outlast any reply letter. The caravan master, now ordered to return, must decide whether to obey or negotiate his own position.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.

seer_journey.purpose: Varek Detained — Listener Below Confirmed in Pre-Founding Records, Kessa Critical
seer_journey.last_outcome: Three pre-founding bore inscriptions name the Listener Below, predating Lostfeld by two centuries. Kessa's fever is spiking. Ironmaul convened the senior three elders at first light. Varek remains formally detained. Ashveil is excluded from the archive session. The question of whether severing the binding will kill Kessa is now the council's primary debate.
